Balancer chromosomes
Marked with dominant homozygous lethals

Major Ideas:
It is possible to put whatever gene you want to, into a fruit fly
It is possible to turn any gene on, in any tissue or even cell that you want to in a fruit fly.
It is possible to visualize any protein you want to in a fruit fly.
It is possible to turn off any gene you want

Gene of interest
Drosophila transformation vector
-a specific Drosophila transposon, called a P-element, allows you to jump genes onto chromosomes
-The transposase has been eliminated to allow element to remain in a stable fashion
P element end                                                          P element end
Mini white-selectable marker for eye color

How to turn on genes?
Promoters
A regulatory region that is a short distance from the 5’ end of a gene and acts as the binding site for RNA polymerase
Enhancers
Enhancer element- A cis-acting regulatory sequence that can elevate levels of transcription from a promotor. Many tissue specific enhancers can act on promotors over many tens of kbs away, and can be 5’, 3’, or within introns of the genes they regulate
With the discovery of enhancers, scientists found that they can identify sequences that turn on genes in specific cells

Tissue-specific
Enhancer

Dominant homozygous lethal traits allow easy detection

The Mouse as a Model System

Key points:
Why use mouse as a genetic model system?
What are the details of the system?

Key points:
Relatively quick generation time for a mammal:
Generation time 6-9 weeks
Similar to humans: Mammals, ~21,000 genes

“Fancy Mice”
Natural variations were collected and traded beginning in the 1700’s in Japan and China
National Mouse Club was founded in Victorian England in 1895
Now over 2,250 different mutant strains
